scoring_methodology: opportunity = demand(0-10) x competition(0-10, higher = less competition) x feasibility(0-10) / 100

ranked_gaps.title: Quantization-Aware Reasoning Evaluation Suite
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An open-source evaluation suite that systematically tests reasoning quality across quantization levels (FP16, INT8, INT4, INT2) on diverse reasoning tasks. Includes multi-step chain degradation analysis, error propagation tracking across reasoning steps, and calibration metrics. Outputs a quantization-reasoning degradation report showing exactly where and how quantization breaks reasoning chains.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Model compression engineers, edge deployment teams, quantization researchers, and r/LocalLLaMA community members who routinely quantize models but have no standardized way to measure reasoning impact. Red Hat ran 500K+ quantized evals and found significant reasoning-specific degradation — practitioners need this daily.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Research in 2025-2026 confirms quantization hurts reasoning disproportionately (OpenReview 'Quantization Hurts Reasoning?' paper, IJCAI 2025 findings). The LocalLLaMA community extensively discusses this but has no tool. W4A16 can be lossless but lower bit-widths introduce accuracy risks that are reasoning-specific. Quantization-aware RL training is now an active research area with no evaluation tooling.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. Core is a test harness that loads models at multiple quantization levels and runs reasoning benchmarks. Can leverage existing lm-eval-harness infrastructure. Main work is curating reasoning-sensitive test cases and building the degradation analysis pipeline.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $200M+ (model optimization tooling market). SAM $30-50M (teams actively quantizing SLMs for production). SOM $3-8M (paid tier for enterprise quantization validation).

ranked_gaps.title: SLM-Specific Reasoning Evaluation Platform with Edge Deployment Metrics
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A unified platform that evaluates SLM reasoning quality jointly with edge deployment constraints: latency budget, memory footprint, thermal throttling, battery drain. Introduces 'reasoning quality per watt' and 'reasoning accuracy at X ms latency budget' as first-class metrics. Integrates with device profilers (Android, iOS, embedded Linux).
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Edge ML engineers, mobile AI developers, IoT AI product teams. AWS is deploying fine-tuned SLMs at the edge for telco (semantic MCP server). On-device LLM adoption in 2026 is surging, but teams lack eval tooling that bridges reasoning and runtime constraints.
ranked_gaps.why_now: SLMBench exists for edge benchmarking (EdgeJSON, EdgeIntent, EdgeFuncCall) but does not measure reasoning. ThinkSLM measures reasoning but ignores edge constraints. 2026 saw explosive growth in on-device LLMs (Edge AI Vision Alliance reports). The ACL 2025 paper 'Demystifying SLMs for Edge Deployment' documents the gap explicitly. No tool fills it yet.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium-High. Requires device-specific profiling integrations (Android Neural Networks API, CoreML, ONNX Runtime). Reasoning benchmarks are available. The hard part is correlating reasoning quality with real-time device telemetry.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $500M+ (edge AI tooling). SAM $40-80M (teams deploying SLMs on-device). SOM $5-12M (platform subscriptions for edge AI teams).

ranked_gaps.title: Low-Cost / Self-Hosted Reasoning Evaluation (No LLM Judge Required)
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A self-hosted reasoning evaluation framework using small verifier models (1-3B parameters) instead of expensive GPT-4/Claude API calls. Leverages Representation-as-a-Judge (INSPECTOR approach) and ThinkPRM-style process verification. Includes pre-trained small verifiers for math, code, and general reasoning with <$0.001 per eval cost.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Startups, indie developers, academic researchers, and budget-constrained teams running continuous evaluation. Current LLM-as-judge costs $900-1800/month for 1000 daily evals. Self-hosted alternative cuts this to near-zero.
ranked_gaps.why_now: 2026 research breakthrough: Representation-as-a-Judge (arXiv 2601.22588) shows small LMs encode rich evaluative signals in hidden states — evaluation needs less semantic capacity than generation. CompassVerifier 3B achieves >90% accuracy. ThinkPRM uses 1% of labels and outperforms LLM-as-Judge. The building blocks exist but no integrated product does.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. CompassVerifier and ThinkPRM provide the model foundation. Need to build: unified API, pre-trained verifier model zoo, integration with popular eval frameworks (lm-eval-harness, DeepEval), and a simple CLI/SDK.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $300M+ (LLM evaluation tooling). SAM $50-80M (teams currently paying for LLM-as-judge). SOM $5-15M (freemium self-hosted + paid cloud tier).

ranked_gaps.title: Contamination-Resistant Dynamic Reasoning Benchmark for SLMs
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A dynamic reasoning benchmark specifically calibrated for SLM capacity ranges (0.5B-7B). Generates novel reasoning problems at difficulty levels matched to model size, ensuring results are informative rather than floor-effect failures. Uses algorithmic problem generation (like BeyondBench) but with SLM-appropriate difficulty tiers and contamination-resistant rolling updates.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: SLM researchers, model trainers, fine-tuning engineers, and benchmark creators. Models below 3B fail most BeyondBench tasks, making results uninformative. Researchers need calibrated difficulty to measure genuine reasoning improvements in small models.
ranked_gaps.why_now: BeyondBench (ICLR 2026) and LiveBench prove contamination-resistant benchmarks work but neither is SLM-calibrated. The 2025-2026 survey on static-to-dynamic evaluation (EMNLP 2025) documents the shift. PACIFIC framework shows automated benchmark generation is mature. The missing piece is SLM-specific difficulty calibration.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. BeyondBench's approach (algorithmic problem generation) is open-source. Need to add: SLM difficulty calibration tiers, rolling update mechanism, and model-size-aware scoring. Can build on top of existing frameworks.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $150M (AI benchmarking market). SAM $20-40M (SLM research teams). SOM $2-5M (benchmark-as-a-service for SLM developers).

ranked_gaps.title: Domain-Specific Reasoning Benchmark Generator for SLMs
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A unified tool that generates domain-specific reasoning evaluation suites for any vertical (legal, medical, financial, scientific) tailored to SLM capabilities. Users provide domain documents/knowledge bases, and the tool auto-generates calibrated reasoning benchmarks with ground-truth verification. Think YourBench but specialized for reasoning tasks and SLM capacity.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Domain-specific AI teams across healthcare, fintech, legal tech. Medical SLMs improve 22% with domain reasoning data (Meerkat). Each vertical currently builds custom evals from scratch — FLAME for finance, Meerkat for medical, but no unified generator exists.
ranked_gaps.why_now: TestAgent (2024) and YourBench (2025) demonstrate auto-generated domain benchmarks are feasible. Fin-PRM shows domain-specific PRMs work. Vertical AI adoption is accelerating in 2026. But existing tools focus on general LLM capabilities, not SLM reasoning in specific domains.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. YourBench provides the zero-shot benchmark generation backbone. Need to add: reasoning-specific task generation, SLM difficulty calibration, domain-specific ground-truth verification, and a library of domain templates (legal, medical, financial).
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $400M+ (vertical AI evaluation). SAM $30-60M (domain-specific AI teams). SOM $3-8M (per-vertical licensing or platform subscriptions).

ranked_gaps.title: SLM Reasoning Comparison Leaderboard with Efficiency Normalization
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A public leaderboard ranking SLMs by reasoning-per-parameter, reasoning-per-FLOP, and reasoning-per-watt. Normalizes scores by model size, inference cost, and deployment constraints so developers can pick the right SLM for their constraint envelope. Includes interactive filtering by parameter count, quantization level, and target hardware.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: ML engineers selecting models, product managers evaluating cost-performance tradeoffs, and cost-conscious AI teams. Currently, developers manually compare BentoML's 'Best SLMs' article or Artificial Analysis leaderboard — neither normalizes for reasoning efficiency.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Smaller models like Nemotron Super 49B achieving frontier-level results shows efficiency is improving rapidly, but no leaderboard tracks this. Multiple leaderboards exist (Onyx, Klu, llm-stats) but none offer per-parameter reasoning normalization. The data exists (ThinkSLM evaluated 72 SLMs) — it just needs proper normalization and a live leaderboard.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Low-Medium. ThinkSLM data provides the foundation. Need: a web frontend, automated evaluation pipeline, efficiency normalization formulas, and community submission. Can start as a static site and iterate.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $100M (developer tools / benchmarking). SAM $10-20M (SLM comparison market). SOM $1-3M (sponsorships, premium API access, enterprise features).

ranked_gaps.title: Multilingual Reasoning Evaluation for SLMs
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A multilingual reasoning benchmark specifically for SLMs, covering 20+ languages with difficulty calibrated to sub-7B model capacity. Includes diagnostic tools to identify where multilingual reasoning breaks down (translation artifacts vs. genuine reasoning failures vs. knowledge gaps). Provides language-specific fine-tuning recommendations.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Global AI product teams, non-English market developers, localization engineers. DeepSeek-R1-7B shows a 35% gap between English and Swahili. Companies deploying SLMs in non-English markets have no way to evaluate multilingual reasoning quality.
ranked_gaps.why_now: MultiNRC (2025) and MMLU-ProX (2025) prove multilingual reasoning benchmarks are needed — but neither is SLM-calibrated. FinMMEval Lab 2026 introduces cross-lingual financial reasoning. The gap between high-resource and low-resource language performance is documented but no SLM-specific tool addresses it.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium-High. Requires multilingual test data curation (expensive for low-resource languages), expert annotation, and SLM-specific difficulty calibration across languages. Can leverage MMLU-ProX translations as starting point.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $250M+ (multilingual AI tooling). SAM $25-50M (non-English SLM deployments). SOM $2-6M (per-language evaluation subscriptions).

ranked_gaps.title: Process Reward Model Evaluation Toolkit for Small Models
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A standalone toolkit for training, evaluating, and deploying small PRMs (1-3B parameters) for step-level reasoning verification. Includes: labeled datasets sized for small model training, evaluation benchmarks (extending ProcessBench for SLMs), and integration with popular RL training loops (TRL, OpenRLHF).
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: RL researchers, SLM alignment teams, reasoning pipeline builders. rStar-Math proved SLMs can use PRMs effectively, and Fin-PRM shows domain-specific PRMs work, but no toolkit makes this accessible for small models.
ranked_gaps.why_now: PRM research exploded in 2025-2026: ThinkPRM (data-efficient), R-PRM (+11.9 F1 on ProcessBench), OR-PRM (operations research), Fin-PRM (finance). Stephen Diehl's open-source PRM training library exists but lacks SLM-specific evaluation. The research is mature enough to productize for small models.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium-High. Requires curating SLM-appropriate training data, building evaluation benchmarks, and integrating with RL training frameworks. Can build on top of sdiehl/prm library and ProcessBench.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $150M (RL/alignment tooling). SAM $15-30M (teams building reasoning pipelines). SOM $2-5M (toolkit licensing + training data).

ranked_gaps.title: Reasoning Chain Visualization and Debugging Tool for SLMs
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An interactive visualization tool for inspecting, debugging, and comparing SLM reasoning chains. Transforms linear CoT text into structured reasoning graphs, highlights failure points, enables step-through debugging, and supports cross-model comparison (e.g., 1.5B vs 3B vs 7B reasoning paths on the same problem).
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Model debuggers, reasoning researchers, prompt engineers. Vis-CoT (2025) proved this concept improves accuracy by 24% on GSM8K but is research-only. No production-ready tool exists for SLM developers.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Vis-CoT (arXiv 2509.01412) demonstrated interactive CoT visualization improves accuracy by up to 24 percentage points. Landscape of Thoughts provides automatic reasoning parsing. CRV enables structural fingerprint analysis. But these are research prototypes, not developer tools. Meanwhile, OpenAI/Google/Anthropic are hiding CoT by default (2026), making open-source visualization for open SLMs more valuable.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. Vis-CoT and Landscape of Thoughts provide the research foundation. Need: production-quality UI (web app), SLM-specific reasoning parsers, cross-model comparison views, and integration with popular inference frameworks.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $200M (developer debugging tools). SAM $15-25M (reasoning researchers and SLM developers). SOM $1-4M (freemium developer tool).

ranked_gaps.title: CI/CD-Native SLM Reasoning Evaluation Pipeline
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A turnkey CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Action + CLI) specifically for SLM reasoning evaluation. Runs a curated subset of reasoning benchmarks in <5 minutes, provides pass/fail gating calibrated for SLM reasoning thresholds, and posts regression reports as PR comments. Leverages tinyBenchmarks (140x speedup) for fast iteration.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: DevOps/MLOps teams, SLM development teams, CI engineers. Promptfoo, Evidently, and Braintrust offer general LLM CI/CD evals but require significant configuration for SLM reasoning. No pre-built pipeline exists for SLM reasoning gates.
ranked_gaps.why_now: CI/CD eval tools matured in 2025-2026: Promptfoo has GitHub Actions, Evidently supports CI/CD, Braintrust auto-comments on PRs. But all are general-purpose LLM tools. tinyBenchmarks enables 140x cost reduction making CI/CD-scale reasoning eval feasible. The building blocks exist — they just need SLM-specific assembly.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Low-Medium. Promptfoo and Evidently provide the CI/CD framework. tinyBenchmarks provides fast evaluation. Need: SLM-specific benchmark selection, reasoning-calibrated gating thresholds, and a pre-built GitHub Action wrapping it all together.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $300M (CI/CD tooling for ML). SAM $20-40M (teams with SLM CI/CD needs). SOM $2-5M (GitHub marketplace + enterprise tier).

ranked_gaps.title: Continuous Reasoning Regression Monitoring for Production SLMs
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A monitoring service that continuously evaluates reasoning quality of production SLMs, detecting silent reasoning degradation from quantization, fine-tuning, or data drift. Sends automatic alerts when reasoning metrics drop below thresholds. Integrates with MLflow, LangSmith, and Datadog.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: MLOps engineers, production AI teams, QA engineers. Quantization preserves 99% accuracy on simple tasks but silently degrades complex reasoning. No monitoring tool catches this.
ranked_gaps.why_now: MLflow and Evidently offer general monitoring. LangSmith supports LangChain apps. But none specialize in reasoning regression for SLMs. As more SLMs enter production in 2026, the need for specialized reasoning monitoring grows.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium-High. Requires: reasoning-specific metric definitions, lightweight continuous evaluation (sampling production traffic), regression detection algorithms, and integrations with major observability platforms.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $250M (AI monitoring). SAM $20-35M (production SLM teams). SOM $2-5M (monitoring SaaS subscriptions).

ranked_gaps.title: Adversarial Reasoning Robustness Toolkit for SLMs
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An integrated toolkit for generating adversarial attacks specifically targeting SLM reasoning chains (not just prompts), evaluating robustness, and providing automated remediation suggestions. Includes reasoning-specific attack generators, robustness scoring, and hardening recommendations.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: AI safety teams, red teams, security engineers, model robustness researchers. ThinkSLM found 11-17% accuracy drops under adversarial perturbation. Intel's LLMart provides general adversarial testing but nothing targets SLM reasoning chains specifically.
ranked_gaps.why_now: LLMart (Intel, 2026) provides a general adversarial robustness toolkit for LLMs. Research shows reasoning models are vulnerable to subtle adversarial inputs targeting safety mechanisms. But no tool focuses on SLM-specific reasoning chain attacks. As SLMs deploy in safety-critical edge environments, this gap becomes urgent.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium-High. Can build on LLMart for attack infrastructure. Need: reasoning-chain-specific attack generators, SLM-calibrated robustness metrics, and automated remediation pipeline.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $200M (AI security tooling). SAM $15-25M (teams red-teaming SLMs). SOM $1-4M (enterprise security subscriptions).

ranked_gaps.title: Cross-Framework Reasoning Evaluation Standardization
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A standardized format and translation layer for cross-tool reasoning evaluation scores. Converts results between OpenCompass, lm-eval-harness, HELM, DeepEval, and Inspect into a common schema. Enables apples-to-apples comparison across frameworks.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Benchmark aggregators, model selectors, research teams comparing results across different evaluation frameworks.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Stanford HELM integrating Unitxt shows recognition of standardization need. 22+ tools with 5+ different scoring methodologies create fragmentation. But adoption requires ecosystem buy-in, making this a slow-burn opportunity.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. Technical implementation is straightforward (schema mapping), but adoption requires community coordination and buy-in from framework maintainers.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $100M (eval tooling interop). SAM $10-15M (multi-framework users). SOM $1-2M (standards body + hosted conversion service).

ranked_gaps.title: Reasoning Evaluation for SLM Ensembles and Multi-Agent Systems
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An evaluation framework for measuring how multiple SLMs reason together in ensemble or multi-agent configurations. Tracks orchestration overhead, consensus quality, collective reasoning accuracy, and identifies when ensemble reasoning fails.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Multi-agent system builders, orchestration framework developers, distributed AI teams. SLM-MUX and LightReasoner show multi-model SLM reasoning works but use ad-hoc evaluation.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Multi-agent AI is trending in 2026 but evaluation tools treat models as isolated units. SLM-MUX demonstrated multi-agent SLM reasoning. This is an emerging area — early but growing.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: High. Multi-agent evaluation is fundamentally harder than single-model eval. Requires defining new metrics, handling non-deterministic orchestration, and supporting diverse agent frameworks.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $150M (multi-agent tooling). SAM $10-20M (multi-agent SLM teams). SOM $1-3M (early-stage, growing).

ranked_gaps.title: Sustainability-Aware Reasoning Evaluation
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An evaluation tool that simultaneously measures reasoning quality and environmental impact (energy consumption, carbon footprint) of SLM inference. Reports 'reasoning quality per kWh' and enables sustainability-conscious model selection.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Sustainability officers, EU-regulated AI teams, green AI researchers. EU AI Act driving regulatory pressure for sustainability reporting on AI systems.
ranked_gaps.why_now: SLM-Bench tracks sustainability but not reasoning. EU AI Act compliance requirements are increasing. However, demand is regulatory-driven (slower adoption) rather than developer-driven.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. SLM-Bench provides sustainability metrics. Need to combine with reasoning benchmarks and build compliance reporting. CodeCarbon and similar tools provide energy measurement foundations.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM $100M (green AI / compliance). SAM $10-20M (EU-regulated AI teams). SOM $1-3M (compliance SaaS).
